D5 Design L5 Evaluate Reference

Rec.709 → P3 LUT

the same RGB code means a different colour in each space — that gap is what a colour-transform LUT exists to close

A code like (0, 200, 0) is not a colour until you say which primaries it rides on. Read it as Rec.709 and you get one green; read the identical code as Display P3 and you get a more saturated one, because P3's primaries sit further out. A LUT rewrites the codes so a clip graded in 709 keeps its intended look on a P3 display.

Slide the source hue of a saturated code value. Both interpretations are plotted on the chromaticity diagram with their gamut triangles, and shown as swatches. The distance between the two points is exactly what a 709→P3 conversion must correct.
